---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 4: Classical ML for Characterization Tasks
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---

## 01. Title: Classical ML for Characterization Tasks

- Unit scope and practical objective.

## 02. Learning outcomes

- Build and evaluate classical baselines.

## 03. Why classical ML still matters

- Strong baselines and interpretability.

## 04. Recap from Units 1–3

- Physics-aware data readiness.

## 05. Task taxonomy

- Classification, regression, clustering.

## 06. Typical characterization targets

- Phase label, defect score, property estimate.

## 07. Feature engineering mindset

- Domain priors before model complexity.

## 08. Hand-crafted feature families

- Intensity, texture, morphology, spectra.

## 09. Feature scaling and normalization

- Pipeline necessity.

## 10. Dimensionality reduction for baselines

- PCA and feature selection.

## 11. Train/val/test strategy refresher

- Grouped and temporal splits.

## 12. Baseline model ladder

- Dummy -> linear -> tree-based.

## 13. Logistic regression

- Interpretable classification baseline.

## 14. Linear regression / ridge / lasso

- Continuous target baselines.

## 15. k-NN intuition

- Local similarity decisions.

## 16. SVM intuition

- Margin-based classification/regression.

## 17. Decision trees

- Rule extraction and overfitting.

## 18. Random forests

- Variance reduction via ensembles.

## 19. Gradient boosting (light)

- Strong tabular baseline.

## 20. Naive Bayes in spectroscopy-like tasks

- Fast probabilistic baseline.

## 21. Hyperparameter tuning basics

- Grid/random search trade-offs.

## 22. Cross-validation under constraints

- GroupKFold/time-aware variants.

## 23. Metric choice for imbalanced classes

- F1, PR-AUC, balanced accuracy.

## 24. Regression metric choices

- MAE, RMSE, R2 caveats.

## 25. Calibration of classifiers

- Reliable probability outputs.

## 26. Threshold optimization

- Cost-sensitive decisions.

## 27. Error analysis workflow

- Slice by instrument/material/process.

## 28. Confusion matrix diagnostics

- Class-specific failure modes.

## 29. Feature importance (model-dependent)

- Tree and linear perspectives.

## 30. Permutation importance

- More robust interpretation.

## 31. SHAP-like intuition (light)

- Local explanation idea.

## 32. Leakage anti-patterns

- Metadata and preprocessing leakage.

## 33. Domain shift risks

- Instrument/site transfer failure.

## 34. Robustness checks

- Perturbation and stress tests.

## 35. Uncertainty proxy strategies

- Ensembles and confidence intervals.

## 36. Reproducible pipeline template

- Fixed seeds, config logging.

## 37. Case sketch: microscopy defect classification

- End-to-end classical pipeline.

## 38. Case sketch: spectrum-based phase ID

- Feature + model scaffold.

## 39. Case sketch: process-quality regression

- Tabular baseline workflow.

## 40. Comparison with deep learning

- When classical methods win.

## 41. MFML dependency map

- Links to probability + optimization.

## 42. Link to Unit 5

- Deep learning for microscopy/spectroscopy.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: define task + split

- Problem framing template.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: feature table

- Build baseline features.

## 45. Exercise scaffold: model benchmark

- Compare 3 classical models.

## 46. Exercise scaffold: tuning + calibration

- Improve one selected model.

## 47. Exercise scaffold: failure analysis

- Diagnose one major error mode.

## 48. Exam-oriented key statements

- High-yield concepts.

## 49. Summary slide

- Core takeaways.

## 50. References + reading assignment

- Prepare deep-learning transition.

::: {#refs}
:::
